How Artificial Intelligence is rewriting the Software Development Life Cycle.
Part 3 of The AI Engineering Playbook
Press enter or click to view image in full size

For the last two years, one question has dominated engineering conferences, startup discussions and technology blogs.
Will AI replace software engineers?
It is a fair question. Every few months another AI model demonstrates a new capability. It builds applications, generates APIs, writes documentation, creates test cases and even fixes production bugs. Modern coding assistants have become remarkably capable, making it easy to believe software engineering itself is becoming obsolete.
I don’t think software engineers are disappearing.
I think software engineering is evolving.
For decades, our industry optimised around one constraint. Writing software was expensive. We created better programming languages, frameworks, development methodologies and cloud platforms because implementation consumed most of a project’s time. Artificial Intelligence changes that equation. When implementation becomes dramatically cheaper, engineers naturally create value somewhere else.
